name: Release a plugin (npm + Docker)
description: Given a git repo URL, clone it, read package.json, publish the package to npm under the @daisy-workflow scope, and build + push a multi-arch Docker image to vivek13186/daisy-<plugin-name>. This is the Daisy-native equivalent of the per-plugin GitHub Actions release pipeline — useful when you want releases coordinated from a single place, or when the plugin repo doesn't have its own CI yet. Trigger via webhook with body { gitUrl, gitRef } or via the Run button in the editor.

nodes.name: cleanWorkdir
nodes.description: Wipe any leftover working directory from a previous run. Idempotent so re-running the workflow doesn't drag stale state forward.

nodes.name: clone
nodes.description: Shallow-clone the plugin repo at the requested ref. The git plugin's clone op takes `dir` (not `directory`); we pass an absolute path so the repo lands exactly at ${workdir} regardless of cwd. depth=1 keeps the clone fast — we only need the working tree for publish, not history.

nodes.name: readPackageJson
nodes.description: Pull the raw package.json off disk so the next node can extract name + version.

nodes.name: parseMetaRaw
nodes.description: Run a tiny node script to derive { name, version, shortName, image } from package.json. Stays in node-land (instead of FEEL string-munging) so the regex-style trim of '@daisy-workflow/' is unambiguous.

args: const p = require(process.argv[1]); const short = p.name.replace('@daisy-workflow/', ''); process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify({ name: p.name, version: p.version, shortName: short, image: 'vivek13186/daisy-' + short }));

nodes.name: meta
nodes.description: Parse the JSON the node script just printed. Trim first because shell.exec preserves trailing newlines.

nodes.name: installDeps
nodes.description: Install runtime deps before publish so the published tarball matches what consumers will install.

nodes.name: npmPublish
nodes.description: Publish to the npm registry. Auth via the `npm` workspace config (token field). --access public is required for scoped packages on the free tier; provenance attestation is GHA-only so we don't pass --provenance here.

nodes.name: dockerLogin
nodes.description: Log in to Docker Hub. Pipe the token via stdin (not argv) so it never shows up in `ps` output. Auth via the `dockerhub` workspace config.

nodes.name: ensureBuilder
nodes.description: Create (or re-use) a buildx builder that supports multi-arch. The docker-container driver is required for cross-platform builds; the default docker driver only builds for the host arch. onError=continue tolerates the case where the builder already exists.

args: docker buildx inspect daisy-release-builder >/dev/null 2>&1 || docker buildx create --name daisy-release-builder --driver docker-container --use; docker buildx use daisy-release-builder; docker buildx inspect --bootstrap

nodes.name: dockerBuildPush
nodes.description: Multi-arch build + push. Tags :<version> AND :latest, with OCI labels so the image self-describes on Docker Hub.

nodes.name: cleanup
nodes.description: Best-effort wipe of the cloned working tree. onError=continue so a release isn't marked failed by a stray cleanup glitch.
